title: Understanding JSX
description: Learn the syntax that mixes JavaScript and markup in React Native.

slides.title: What is JSX? 🤔
slides.content:
# JSX = JavaScript + XML

JSX is a syntax extension that lets you write HTML‑like markup inside JavaScript. It's not valid JavaScript – it gets transformed by **Babel** into `React.createElement` calls.

## Example

```jsx
const element = <Text>Hello</Text>;
```

After transformation:

```js
const element = React.createElement(Text, null, 'Hello');
```

JSX makes your code much more readable and expressive.

slides.title: JSX Rules 📏
Embedding Expressions 🧮
slides.content: You can embed any JavaScript expression inside curly braces `{}`.
slides.codeSnippet:
const name = 'Alice';
const age = 25;

<View>
  <Text>Hello, {name}!</Text>
  <Text>You are {age} years old.</Text>
  <Text>In 5 years you'll be {age + 5}.</Text>
</View>

quizData.question: What is the correct way to embed a JavaScript variable inside JSX?
quizData.explanation: Single curly braces `{}` are used to evaluate JavaScript expressions inside JSX.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Use an **online JSX transformer** (like the Babel REPL) to see how JSX turns into plain JavaScript. It demystifies the magic!
